title: Intensely Savory Korean Mussels Stew
description: A savory and crumbly Korean dish featuring mussels, perfect for a date night, originating from Busan, and sugar-free-friendly. This stew is made with a variety of spices and ingredients to create a rich and intense flavor.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the gochujang and soy sauce, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the mussels to the pot, cover with a lid, and cook for 5 minutes, or until the mussels are opened.
Remove the pot from the heat and stir in the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds.
Serve the mussels and sauce in bowls, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Use a bain-marie to keep the stew warm while serving.
Discard any unopened mussels before serving.
